crux_statement: The rating on onsemi reduces to one question: can the SiC power device business recover from its ~$1B trough back toward $3.5-4B by FY2027-28, and does the AI datacenter power pivot (GaNEXUS/SiC/Treo) supply a credible second growth engine large enough to bridge the cycle? At $121.62 the market prices roughly $8-9B revenue at 18-20% operating margin by FY2028 — a near-full return to FY2023 peak economics plus the AI increment. The SiC recovery pace (capacity utilisation, competitive share, EV demand) and the AI datacenter revenue ramp rate are the two variables that decide whether the current 28x NTM P/E is cheap or full. Everything else — ISG imaging, AMG legacy analog, capital allocation — is second-order.

crux_variable.label: SiC revenue by FY2027 (the primary lever) and AI datacenter revenue by FY2027 (the second vector)
crux_variable.identity: onsemi SiC revenue = [SiC addressable market (EV TAM × SiC content per vehicle × EV penetration rate)] × [onsemi market share of SiC device market]
crux_variable.identity_ai_dc: AI datacenter revenue = [hyperscaler power-semi spend pool] × [onsemi wallet share across power tree: SiC JFET + GaN + Treo PMICs]
crux_variable.sic_ceiling_fy2027: $4.5B (total SiC device market at ~$6.5B × onsemi at max credible 30% share per Infineon's own stated target ceiling for itself)
crux_variable.sic_floor_fy2027: $1.2B (demand trough maintains, cycle does not recover; onsemi's FY2025 level ~$1B is the realized trough floor)

demand_nature.near_term_contracted: LTA-backed backlog with ZEEKR, VW Group, BMW, NIO, Li Auto, Hyundai — these are signed multi-year supply agreements. The first search found $4.5B+ of committed SiC revenue as of Q4 2022; these LTAs underpin FY2023's ~$800M actual SiC revenue and the ramp into FY2024. They do NOT guarantee revenue if customers draw down inventory rather than take deliveries — which is exactly what happened in FY2024-2025 (customers had over-ordered, forcing demand pause).
demand_nature.extrapolated: The $3B+ FY2027 base SiC path requires EV demand to resume normal growth trajectories (global EV share ~27-32% by 2028 per BNEF/IEA), onsemi's Czech Republic SiC fab to ramp successfully (first qualified 200mm wafers late 2026, volume production 2027), and onsemi to hold ~22-25% SiC market share against Infineon's 200mm cost push and Chinese domestic displacement.
demand_nature.hoped_for: The $3.8B bull SiC path requires onsemi to gain share FROM STMicro (the current #1 at 32.6% share) via design-win momentum (55% of new EV model wins at 2026 Beijing Auto Show is the leading indicator) AND for EV demand growth to accelerate back toward 30%+ annual growth, which would require faster-than-consensus hybrid-to-BEV conversion in China.

crux_base.label: BASE
crux_base.argument: The base reflects the honest most-likely: SiC recovers as the EV inventory correction clears (it is already clearing -- Q1 2026 automotive revenue was +14% YoY for PSG, first YoY growth in 7 quarters) and EV volumes resume 15-20% annual growth globally (IEA Global EV Outlook 2026: 23.3M EVs in 2026 vs ~20M in 2025, rising to ~31% global share by 2028). onsemi holds approximately 22-25% SiC device market share (TrendForce, June 2024 -- the last reliable published figure), consistent with its #2 rank. The SiC device market grows from ~$2.7B in 2025 (Mordor Intelligence) toward ~$5-6B by 2027 at the lower-to-mid end of YOLE's 25% CAGR forecast (tempered by the EV slowdown deferring some demand to 2026-2027). At 22-23% share of a ~$5.5B market, onsemi's SiC revenue reaches ~$1.2-1.3B in 2025 (consistent with the inference that ~$800M in 2023 with ~25% share implied ~$3.2B total market), growing to ~$3.0B in 2027 at ~23% share of a ~$6.5B total market. The Czech Republic 200mm SiC fab ramps gradually from late 2026 but does NOT reach full utilisation in the 2026-2027 window, so gross margin recovery is partial -- to 41-44% range rather than the 47-52% structural target. AI datacenter doubles again in 2026 (management guidance: FY2026 doubling from $250M FY2025 base = ~$500M), then grows 30% in 2027 as GaNEXUS design funnel ($1.5B as of June 2026) converts to revenue. The base implies $7.8B total company revenue by FY2027 at ~43% gross margin, generating ~$4.50-5.00 non-GAAP EPS. This is ~the street's recovery consensus ($4.50-5.50 range) and is a REALISTIC, NON-OPTIMISTIC scenario -- it requires no share gains and no market beats, just a cycle recovery at the prevailing competitive position.

key_evidence.source: IEA Global EV Outlook 2026 (23.3M EVs in 2026, ~27.4% share 2026 rising to 31.8% by 2028)

key_evidence.claim: SiC device market ~$2.7B in 2025 growing to ~$8.4B by 2030 at 25.7% CAGR
key_evidence.source: Mordor Intelligence Silicon Carbide Power Semiconductor Market report

key_evidence.claim: YOLE: SiC power semiconductor market $8.9B by 2028 at 31% CAGR from 2022 base
key_evidence.source: YOLE Intelligence Power SiC Markets 2022-2028 report, cited by Powerwaywafer.com

key_evidence.claim: onsemi SiC market share ~22-25%, ranked #2 after STMicro (32.6%) as of 2023
AI datacenter revenue: $250M+ in FY2025, management guiding for doubling in FY2026
key_evidence.source: onsemi Q4 2025 earnings call (Feb 2026) and Q1 2026 earnings call (May 4, 2026)

key_evidence.claim: Q1 2026 PSG revenue $737M (+14% YoY), first YoY growth in 7 quarters
55% share of new EV model wins at 2026 Beijing Auto Show
key_evidence.source: onsemi Q1 2026 earnings call management statement

crux_base.value: $85-100 per share (implied: $7.8B revenue x 18% op margin = ~$1.4B EBIT; at 22-24x on ~$5.00 non-GAAP EPS, normalised FY2027 basis)

crux_bull.label: BULL
crux_bull.argument: The bull case rests on two compounding forces: (1) onsemi gains SiC share from STMicro. The design-win signal is unusually strong: 55% of new EV model wins at the 2026 Beijing Auto Show, up from an estimated 35-40% in 2023-2024. The share-of-wins metric leads revenue by 12-24 months (design-in to ramp). If onsemi's design share converts to 28-30% SiC revenue share in 2027-2028 (vs 22-25% today), and the total SiC device market hits the upper end of YOLE's forecast (~$8B+ by 2028), onsemi's SiC revenue reaches $2.3-2.5B by 2028 from automotive alone. (2) The AI datacenter vector is real and large. onsemi confirmed >30 active programs across every major hyperscaler (Q1 2026 earnings), with content per 1MW AI rack doubling from $50,000 to $100,000 as architectures shift to 800VDC. The $1.5B GaN design funnel (June 2026 GaNEXUS launch) and Qorvo SiC JFET acquisition (December 2025, $115M) position onsemi for the full DC power tree, not just a single socket. If AI datacenter revenue reaches $1B in FY2027 (4x FY2025's $250M), total company revenue approaches $9B at structurally higher gross margins (~47% as mix shifts to high-ASP GaN and SiC traction modules). Wolfspeed's bankruptcy (filed June 2025, emerged September 2025 with $4.6B debt eliminated) is a net positive: it removes the most aggressive pure-play device competitor and reduces substrate competition, improving onsemi's vertically-integrated SiC cost position as its Czech Republic fab ramps. The EU approved a €450M grant for the Czech Republic fab in December 2025, locking in the strategic investment. Bull non-GAAP EPS trajectory: ~$6.50-7.50 by FY2028, at 22-24x = $140-180 per share.

key_evidence.claim: 55% share of new EV model design wins at 2026 Beijing Auto Show vs prior estimated 35-40% share
GaNEXUS design funnel >$1.5B at launch (June 2026); 40-650V range; >30 active AI datacenter programs
Power GaN device market growing at 42% CAGR to $3B by 2030 per YOLE
Wolfspeed emerged from Chapter 11 with $4.6B debt eliminated (June-September 2025); onsemi's internal SiC substrate advantage strengthened
EU approved €450M grant for onsemi Czech Republic 200mm SiC fab (December 2025)
Infineon's own stated SiC market share target is 30% by 2030 -- implying remaining 70% is contested market

crux_bull.evidence_quality: EXTRAPOLATED — the design-win share (55%) is contracted/observed, but the revenue conversion relies on EV demand growing and Treo/GaNEXUS design funnel converting at historical rates. Not yet CONTRACTED.

crux_bear.label: BEAR
crux_bear.argument: The bear case is structurally grounded, not tail risk. It rests on four mechanisms: (1) INFINEON 200mm COST DISPLACEMENT: Infineon began customer rollout of 200mm SiC products from Villach in February 2025 and is ramping its Kulim Malaysia 200mm fab through 2026, with a stated target of 30% SiC market share by 2030. At 200mm, Infineon's cost per device falls ~30-40% vs 150mm (larger wafer area, same process steps). If Infineon's unit cost in 2027-2028 undercuts onsemi's 150mm device by ~15-25%, Tier 1 automotive suppliers (Bosch, Continental) may re-source -- particularly for European OEM platforms where Infineon has existing MCU relationships. onsemi's Czech Republic 200mm fab does not reach volume production until 2027 at the earliest (EU grant received December 2025; first-wafer milestones expected late 2026), leaving an 18-24 month window where Infineon has a cost advantage onsemi cannot match. (2) CHINESE DOMESTIC DISPLACEMENT: China is onsemi's highest-growth SiC region and its most contested. Chinese EV OEMs (BYD, Nio, ZEEKR, Li Auto) are simultaneously onsemi customers AND subject to government pressure to source domestically. SICC, Sanan Optoelectronics, TankeBlue, and BYD Semiconductor are scaling 150mm and 200mm SiC production with government support; S&P Global (September 2025) documented the China automotive industry's active semiconductor self-reliance push. Even if onsemi retains design wins on current models, NEW Chinese EV programs may specify domestic SiC in 2026-2027. (3) EV DEMAND STRUCTURAL DECELERATION: The hybrid vehicle (PHEV/HEV) segment is winning market share over pure BEV in several developed markets (US: EV share only 8.7% in 2026 per BNEF; Europe growing but below prior expectations). HEV powertrains use SiC but at LOWER content per vehicle (~$15,000-25,000 per HEV inverter vs $115,000 for 800V BEV) because lower voltage architecture. If the BEV adoption curve is flatter than YOLE's base case, the SiC device market grows more slowly than forecast -- perhaps $4-5B by 2027 rather than $6-8B. At 22% share, onsemi's SiC revenue is $880M-1.1B in 2027, not $3B+. (4) CAPACITY IMPAIRMENT SIGNAL: onsemi took $600-700M in manufacturing equipment impairment charges in Q1 2025 (March 17, 2025) and a further $200-300M in November 2025 -- totalling $800M-1B in admitted overcapacity writedowns. Management characterized this as 'realigning internal manufacturing capacity with anticipated long-term needs.' This is a direct admission that the Czech Republic and other SiC fab investments exceeded actual demand, and that the recovery pace is slower than the $4B+ LTA backlog from 2022 implied. The impairments are the most credible bear signal in the public record -- management itself has confirmed that some of the capacity is no longer economic at today's demand levels. Bear EPS: ~$2.50-3.00 non-GAAP in FY2027 (trough-plus recovery with structurally impaired margins from idle capacity), at 18-20x = $45-60 per share.

key_evidence.claim: Infineon began 200mm SiC product customer rollout from Villach in February 2025; Kulim Malaysia fab ramping to high-volume production
key_evidence.source: Infineon press release February 2025; Compound Semiconductor News

key_evidence.claim: Infineon targets 30% SiC global market share by 2030 -- explicitly positioning to displace current #1 (STMicro 32.6%) and #2 (onsemi 22-25%)
China domestic SiC suppliers (Sanan, SICC, TankeBlue, BYD Semiconductor) scaling with government support; S&P Global documents active semiconductor self-reliance push
key_evidence.source: S&P Global Automotive Insights, September 2025; Digitimes

key_evidence.claim: onsemi recognized $600-700M in manufacturing equipment impairment charges (March 2025) + $200-300M additional (November 2025) = ~$800M-1B total capacity overcapacity admission
key_evidence.source: onsemi 8-K filings, March 17, 2025 and November 13, 2025; SEC EDGAR

key_evidence.claim: US BEV share only 8.7% in 2026; European EV below prior expectations; hybrid (lower SiC content) gaining vs pure BEV
key_evidence.source: BNEF Electric Vehicle Outlook 2026; FastMarkets 2026 EV trends

key_evidence.claim: STMicro SiC revenue reached $1.1-1.2B in 2024 despite EV demand softness; ST aggressively cut costs and targets its own Italian 200mm fab by 2026

crux_bear.evidence_quality: OBSERVABLE-AND-NEAR (impairments, Infineon 200mm ramp) + MECHANISM (Chinese domestic displacement, cost competition)

competitors.name: STMicroelectronics
competitors.sic_revenue_2024: ~$1.1-1.2B (revised down from $1.3B guidance during 2024 EV demand correction)
competitors.trajectory: DEFENDING. STMicro lost ground to onsemi in 2023 (design-win momentum shifted) but retains largest revenue share. Building 200mm SiC fab in Catania, Italy targeting 2026 operational. Geely Auto LTA signed. Revenue declined in 2024 alongside onsemi due to same EV inventory correction. Pricing pressure to defend share. Rating: HOLD/NEUTRAL.
competitors.moat: European OEM relationships (Stellantis collaboration announced November 2024 with Infineon, not ST -- but ST has Ferrari, European Tier 1 exposure); 4th generation STPOWER SiC MOSFET technology launched September 2024.

competitors.name: Infineon Technologies
competitors.position: #3 SiC market share (2023); aggressively moving to #1 target by 2030
competitors.share_source: TrendForce June 2024 (combined with Mordor Intelligence estimates)
competitors.sic_revenue_2024: ~$1.5B (Infineon Power and Discrete segment $3.13B total; SiC estimated ~$1.5B; automotive total >$8B)
competitors.trajectory: GAINING. Most dangerous bear catalyst. Began 200mm SiC product rollout from Villach February 2025; Kulim Malaysia 200mm fab entering high-volume production ramp 2026. Stated target: 30% SiC global market share by 2030 -- which would make it the #1 player. Acquired GaN Systems in 2023, competing directly with onsemi's GaNEXUS in AI datacenter. Stellantis multi-year SiC supply agreement (November 2024). Cost position from 200mm will be structurally superior to 150mm competitors through 2027.
competitors.moat: Scale ($15B+ annual revenue), existing automotive MCU relationships (every Tier 1 uses Infineon MCUs -- lock-in), 200mm SiC first-mover cost advantage, CoolSiC brand loyalty at European OEMs.

competitors.name: Wolfspeed
competitors.position: #4 SiC device market; #1 SiC substrate supplier (33.7% substrate market share, TrendForce 2024)
competitors.trajectory: RESTRUCTURED. Filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy June 30, 2025; emerged September 29, 2025 after 91-day prepack eliminating $4.6B debt. Annual interest expense cut ~60%. 200mm SiC fab in Chatham County NC intact. NET IMPACT ON ONSEMI: Mixed. Wolfspeed's distress removes the most aggressive pure-play device competitor (positive for onsemi market position) but creates substrate supply uncertainty for competitors who purchase Wolfspeed substrates. Onsemi's vertical integration (own crystal growth) is a competitive advantage here.
competitors.moat: Substrate supply dominance (33.7% market share); Durham NC device heritage; restructured balance sheet post-bankruptcy with reduced fixed costs.

competitors.name: Chinese SiC suppliers (SICC, Sanan, TankeBlue, BYD Semiconductor)
competitors.share_source: S&P Global Automotive Insights September 2025; Digitimes
competitors.trajectory: GAINING IN CHINA. Government-backed semiconductor self-reliance push actively redirecting Chinese EV OEM procurement. SICC partnered with Bosch; TankeBlue signed with Infineon. Device consumption in China estimated USD 1.4-1.7B in 2026 (IndexBox), ~50-55% of Asia-Pacific revenue. Chinese players are the primary bear mechanism in the China EV market, which is onsemi's largest SiC growth region. Not yet a threat in European/US automotive OEM platforms.
competitors.moat: Government support, local supply chain for China EV OEMs, rapidly scaling 6-inch capacity at lower cost.

competitors.name: Texas Instruments
competitors.position: Primary rival in analog/mixed-signal (AMG competition); not a primary SiC competitor
competitors.trajectory: STABLE IN ANALOG. TI does not compete directly in SiC power devices but is onsemi's primary competitor for the Treo platform's addressable market (the $36B analog/mixed-signal TAM). TI's scale, fab ownership, and breadth of portfolio makes it the entrenched incumbent in industrial and automotive analog -- onsemi's Treo must displace TI parts in design-ins. GaN: TI has mature GaN for data center power (LMG5200 family), competing against GaNEXUS.
competitors.moat: Industry's most comprehensive analog portfolio, 14+ internal fabs, decades of customer design-in lock-in in industrial analog.

sic_total_device_market_2024_est.note: 2024 saw market contraction vs 2023 due to EV inventory correction; YOLE cited $3.2B+ in 2023, with 2024 flat to slightly down

sic_total_device_market_2027_forecast.source_mid: YOLE $8.9B by 2028 implies ~$6-7B by 2027 at 25-31% CAGR; blended estimate
sic_total_device_market_2027_forecast.source_high: Fortune Business Insights / Meticulous Research upper end
sic_total_device_market_2027_forecast.triangulation_note: Three houses give 2028/2030 forecasts ranging $8.4B-10.3B; the range for 2027 is $5.5-8B. The dispersion is real and reflects genuine EV demand uncertainty. The midpoint at ~$6.5B is the base case.

addressable_market.sic_automotive_share: ~62% of SiC devices (Mordor Intelligence 2024); expected to remain dominant through 2028 as 800V EV architecture drives content per vehicle
addressable_market.onsemi_addressable_slice: onsemi targets automotive EV inverters (main) + OBCs + DC-DC + industrial motor drives + AI datacenter power. Automotive SiC alone is ~$4B of the $6.5B 2027 market; onsemi's design-win position gives it access to all segments but China domestic increasingly contested.

onsemi_current_capture.revenue_est_2025: ~$0.9-1.1B SiC device revenue (estimated from PSG $2.9B 2025 revenue at ~35-40% SiC share; PSG also includes legacy silicon power, IGBTs)
onsemi_current_capture.note: onsemi does not separately disclose SiC revenue quarterly. The $0.9-1.1B 2025 estimate is inferred from: (a) $800M 2023 SiC actual (per YoleGroup estimate) x approximate decline rates consistent with PSG revenue decline of ~24% FY2023-2025.

gan_ai_dc_market.2026_est: $0.3-0.5B addressable for onsemi's voltage range (40-650V targeting AI rack power delivery)
gan_ai_dc_market.2028_est: $1.0-2.0B (YOLE: power GaN $3B by 2030 at 42% CAGR from 2025 base)
gan_ai_dc_market.onsemi_wallet_share_today: ~30-35% of hyperscaler XPU vendor programs engaged (>30 active programs per Q1 2026 earnings call)

share_dynamic.summary: SiC is a three-player market in transition: STMicro (#1 at ~33%) defends; onsemi (#2 at ~22-25%) has design-win momentum to take share; Infineon (#3 at ~15-18%) is the structural threat with 200mm cost leadership arriving 2026-2027. Chinese domestic players are a China-specific bear. The share-of-wins metric (onsemi at 55% of new EV model wins at 2026 Beijing Auto Show) leads revenue share by 12-24 months -- if it holds, onsemi's revenue share rises from ~22% toward 28-30% by 2028. But the conversion depends on EV demand growing, not just design-win rates.
share_dynamic.on_share_2024: ~22-25% (maintained; EV downturn hit all players proportionally)
share_dynamic.on_share_2027e_base: ~22-24% (flat; share gains from design-wins offset by Infineon cost pressure)
share_dynamic.on_share_2027e_bull: ~27-30% (design-win momentum converts; STMicro pricing pressure loses European customers to onsemi or Infineon)
share_dynamic.on_share_2027e_bear: ~17-20% (Infineon 200mm wins European OEM sockets; Chinese players take China onsemi share)
share_dynamic.st_share_2024: ~28-32% (revenue declined faster than market in 2024; some share loss evident from $1.1B actual vs $1.3B+ expected)
share_dynamic.chinese_players_2026: ~8-12% combined SiC device market (predominantly in China; growing 30-40% annually)
share_dynamic.share_shift_mechanism: The share shift FROM STMicro TO onsemi in 2022-2023 was driven by: (a) onsemi's aggressive LTA campaign securing design commitments before capacity was built; (b) onsemi's JFET technology differentiation for OBC/DC-DC stages; (c) STMicro's concentration in a few large customers (Tesla, European OEMs) that reduced orders during the inventory correction. The next shift IN 2026-2028 will be driven by 200mm cost -- the first producer to volume 200mm at competitive yield wins the next wave of Tier 1 automotive qualifications. Infineon is 12-18 months ahead of onsemi on 200mm. This is the key contested variable.
share_dynamic.source: TrendForce June 2024; Mordor Intelligence; Infineon investor communications; onsemi earnings; S&P Global

crux_decomposition.identity: onsemi total revenue = PSG(SiC) + PSG(silicon) + AMG(Treo+legacy) + ISG(automotive imaging)

lever_1_sic_market_size.driver: Global EV sales volume x SiC content per EV (rising with 800V architecture penetration)
lever_1_sic_market_size.trajectory: Growing 20-30% CAGR to $6-9B by 2028 (YOLE, Mordor, triangulated)
lever_1_sic_market_size.contested: CONTESTED -- EV adoption pace (especially US/Europe BEV vs hybrid split) determines upper bound

lever_2_on_market_share.driver: Design-win rate (leading indicator: 55% of 2026 Beijing Auto Show wins) vs Infineon 200mm cost competition (lagging threat: 2027-2028 volume)
lever_2_on_market_share.trajectory: Bull: rising to 28-30% as design-wins convert; Bear: falling to 17-20% as Infineon undercuts on 200mm cost
lever_2_on_market_share.contested: CONTESTED -- most important single variable for the call

lever_3_ai_dc_revenue.driver: Hyperscaler capex growth x onsemi wallet share of power tree (SiC JFET + GaN + Treo PMICs)
lever_3_ai_dc_revenue.trajectory: Management guiding to doubling in FY2026; GaNEXUS $1.5B design funnel supports further growth; YOLE power GaN $3B by 2030 at 42% CAGR
lever_3_ai_dc_revenue.contested: EXTRAPOLATED -- Q1 2026 +30% QoQ momentum is real; design funnel is real; but funnel-to-revenue conversion rate is uncertain

lever_4_gross_margin.driver: SiC fab utilisation (Czech Republic ramp) + product mix shift (GaN/SiC higher margin vs legacy silicon) + 200mm cost reduction (2027-2028)
lever_4_gross_margin.current_level: 38.5% non-GAAP (Q1 2026) -- third consecutive quarterly expansion from 33% trough
lever_4_gross_margin.trajectory: Base: 41-44% by FY2027 (partial fab utilisation recovery); Bull: 45-48% (Czech fab ramps, GaN mix); Bear: 37-39% (competitive pricing limits recovery)
lever_4_gross_margin.contested: CONTESTED -- the single biggest swing on EPS; 1pp gross margin change on $8B revenue = ~$80M EBIT = ~$0.20 EPS

bias_audit.check: Reviewing for stacked lean per CONTRACT §1.3
findings: SiC market size: Using midpoint of YOLE/Mordor range ($6.5B 2027) -- not the lower end. NEUTRAL.
Market share: Using flat 22-24% in base (no share gain assumed). Conservative vs design-win signal but defensible given Infineon threat. SLIGHT CONSERVATIVE LEAN.
AI datacenter: Using $650M base for FY2027 (2.6x FY2025 $250M). Management guidance implies $500M FY2026; $650M FY2027 implies only 30% growth. Reasonable. NEUTRAL.
Gross margin: Using 43% base for FY2027 (below FY2023 peak of 47%). Given impairments and capacity utilisation uncertainty, this is the honest center. NEUTRAL.
Overall lean count: 1 slight conservative, 0 slight optimistic. No stacked bias. Base is the honest most-likely.
bias_audit.verdict: NO STACKED BIAS DETECTED. The base is defensible at its own honest center.
